attractions.name: Sacred Monkey Forest Sanctuary
attractions.description: A nature reserve and temple complex home to hundreds of long-tailed macaques and ancient banyan trees.

attractions.name: Ubud Royal Palace (Puri Saren Agung)
attractions.description: The historic residence of the Ubud royal family, featuring beautiful traditional Balinese architecture.

attractions.name: Goa Gajah (Elephant Cave)
attractions.description: An 9th-century archaeological site featuring a cave entrance carved into a demon's mouth and ancient bathing pools.

attractions.name: Gunung Kawi Temple
attractions.description: A stunning 11th-century temple complex with ten rock-cut shrines carved into the cliff face.

attractions.name: Tirta Empul Temple
attractions.description: A famous Hindu Balinese water temple known for its holy spring water used for ritual purification.

attractions.name: Campuhan Ridge Walk
attractions.description: A scenic, paved hiking trail through lush green hills and valleys, perfect for sunrise or sunset.

attractions.name: Tegalalang Rice Terrace
attractions.description: Iconic terraced rice paddies offering breathtaking views and traditional irrigation systems.

attractions.name: Museum Puri Lukisan
attractions.description: The oldest art museum in Ubud, showcasing a vast collection of modern Balinese paintings and woodcarvings.

restaurants.name: Locavore
restaurants.description: Renowned fine-dining restaurant focusing on locally sourced, seasonal ingredients.

restaurants.name: Naughty Nuri's Ubud
restaurants.description: Famous for its signature pork ribs and vibrant, casual atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Clear Cafe
restaurants.description: A unique, health-conscious spot with stunning architecture and a wide range of organic dishes.

restaurants.name: Warung Makan Bu Rus
restaurants.description: Authentic Balinese home-style cooking served in a beautiful garden setting.

hidden_gems.name: Pura Dalem Puri Peliatan
hidden_gems.description: A quiet, atmospheric temple often missed by the main tourist crowds, offering a glimpse into local spiritual life.

nightlife.name: CP Lounge
nightlife.description: A popular late-night spot with live music, pool tables, and a dance floor.

shopping.name: Ubud Art Market
shopping.description: The central hub for traditional crafts, textiles, and souvenirs.

local_tips: Hire a local driver for a full day to explore temples outside the town center.
Always carry a sarong in your bag for spontaneous temple visits.
Traffic in central Ubud is heavy; walking is often faster than taking a car.
Avoid the peak heat by starting your sightseeing early in the morning.

safety.general: Ubud is generally safe, but be cautious of pickpockets in crowded markets.
safety.water_safety: Do not drink tap water; use bottled or filtered water.
safety.transport_safety: Be careful when walking on narrow roads with heavy motorbike traffic.

culture.overview: Ubud is the cultural heart of Bali, deeply rooted in Hindu traditions and arts.
culture.etiquette: Always dress modestly when visiting temples (shoulders and knees covered). Never point your feet at people or religious objects.

transport.getting_around: Walking is best for the center. For outskirts, use local drivers or rent a motorbike.
transport.airport: Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS) is about 1.5-2 hours away by car.
